title: Project Report: Real-Time Dashboard with WebSockets — Frontend React Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: React dashboard with live metrics via WebSocket, Recharts visualizations, auth, and error boundaries.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Advanced)*

**Real-Time Dashboard with WebSockets** is a advanced-level end-to-end project for Frontend React Developer. Duration: 4–5 weeks. React dashboard with live metrics via WebSocket, Recharts visualizations, auth, and error boundaries. Tools: React 19, Recharts, WebSocket, JWT.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: WebSocket live data feed
Objective: Recharts time-series charts
Objective: Auth-protected routes
Objective: Error boundaries + retry logic
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Frontend React Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Frontend React Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Frontend React Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.title: Phase: WS Client
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: WS Client** *(Level: Advanced)*

Custom useWebSocket hook. Complete these tasks: Reconnect logic; Heartbeat. Deliverable for this phase: Hook unit tests.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] Reconnect logic
- [ ] Heartbeat
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### What it actually is
Phase WS Client is one step in the full project lifecycle.

### When to use it

### Where to use it

### Real use example
Team demo shows the Hook unit tests to the teacher.

```text
- [ ] Fallback UI
- [ ] Sentry integration
```

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Advanced)*

Submit all final deliverables: Live dashboard; Architecture doc; Error handling demo; Performance profile. Skills demonstrated: WebSockets, Charts, Auth. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: Live dashboard
Deliverable: Architecture doc
Deliverable: Error handling demo
Deliverable: Performance profile
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits Live dashboard and passes the rubric.
